description: Enable simultaneous multi-model agent execution across Claude and Codex (OpenAI), where Codex runs in Linux containers. Three pillars: (1) Fix ams_common.py so the Rust kernel binary is found on Linux (no .exe extension). (2) Fix Codex skills so they use ./scripts/ams instead of scripts\ams.bat on Linux. (3) Add a Codex agent driver to run-swarm-plan.py so the orchestrator can spawn both Claude and Codex workers in the same swarm run. Nodes 01-03 are independent; node 04 depends on 03; node 05 is independent.

nodes.title: 01-fix-linux-binary-detection
nodes.description:
Fix `scripts/ams_common.py` so the Rust `ams-core-kernel` binary is located correctly on Linux containers, and so the default AMS command label shows the right wrapper for the current platform.

## Files to change
`scripts/ams_common.py`

## Changes required

### 1. Platform-aware RUST_AMS_EXE constant
The current line:
  RUST_AMS_EXE = RUST_AMS_KERNEL_DIR / "target" / "release" / "ams-core-kernel.exe"
is Windows-only. Replace it with two constants and make `build_rust_ams_cmd` check both:

```python
import sys as _sys
RUST_AMS_EXE_WIN   = RUST_AMS_KERNEL_DIR / "target" / "release" / "ams-core-kernel.exe"
RUST_AMS_EXE_LINUX = RUST_AMS_KERNEL_DIR / "target" / "release" / "ams-core-kernel"
```

In `build_rust_ams_cmd`, check `RUST_AMS_EXE_WIN` first (preserves existing Windows behaviour), then `RUST_AMS_EXE_LINUX`, then fall through to `cargo run`:

```python
def build_rust_ams_cmd(*args: str) -> list[str] | None:
    if RUST_AMS_EXE_WIN.exists():
        return [str(RUST_AMS_EXE_WIN), *args]
    if RUST_AMS_EXE_LINUX.exists():
        return [str(RUST_AMS_EXE_LINUX), *args]
    cargo_toml = RUST_AMS_KERNEL_DIR / "Cargo.toml"
    if cargo_toml.exists():
        return ["cargo", "run", "--release", "--", *args]
    return None
```

### 2. Platform-aware DEFAULT_AMS_MEMORY_CMD
The current line:
  DEFAULT_AMS_MEMORY_CMD = r"scripts\ams.bat"
is used only for display in `memory_command_label()` (the actual invocation goes through `build_ams_wrapper_cmd` which is already platform-aware). Change it to:

```python
DEFAULT_AMS_MEMORY_CMD = (
    r"scripts\ams.bat" if _sys.platform == "win32" else "./scripts/ams"
)
```

Note: `_sys` is `import sys as _sys` which you must add near the top of the file if not already present. The file already has `import sys` at line 4 - use `sys` directly instead: `"scripts\\ams.bat" if sys.platform == "win32" else "./scripts/ams"`

### 3. Remove stale RUST_AMS_EXE references
After adding the two new constants, remove or replace any remaining references to the old `RUST_AMS_EXE` name anywhere in ams_common.py (grep for it first). If `RUST_AMS_EXE` is imported by other scripts (ams.py, run-swarm-plan.py), update those imports too or add a compatibility alias: `RUST_AMS_EXE = RUST_AMS_EXE_WIN` at the bottom of the constants section.

## Acceptance
- `python scripts/ams.py --help` must still succeed on Windows after the change.
- `grep -n RUST_AMS_EXE scripts/ams_common.py` must show the renamed constants (WIN/LINUX) and any compat alias, not the old bare name.
- `grep -rn RUST_AMS_EXE scripts/` should not show any broken imports.

nodes.title: 02-fix-codex-skill-linux-compat
nodes.description:
Update the Codex skill files so agents in Linux containers are given Unix-compatible commands, not Windows-style `scripts\ams.bat` paths.

## Files to change

### codex-skills/ams/SKILL.md
The file currently only shows Windows commands. Add a Unix block after the Windows block so that:
- The Windows block is labelled `Windows:`
- A new `Unix/container:` block follows, showing `./scripts/ams <verb>` equivalents for every command in the Windows block.
Keep the existing structure and rules sections intact.

### codex-skills/ams/agents/openai.yaml
The `default_prompt` field currently says something like:
  "Use the repo AMS wrapper for memory lookup. Start with scripts\ams.bat search, ..."
Change it to be platform-neutral:
  "Use the repo AMS wrapper for memory lookup. On Windows: `scripts\\ams.bat search \"<keywords>\"`. On Linux/container: `./scripts/ams search \"<keywords>\"`. Then use thread, handoff, sessions, or read as needed."

### codex-skills/ams/references/cli-map.md
The file shows only Windows commands under each verb. After the existing Windows examples for each verb (search, read, sessions, thread, handoff), add a Unix/container equivalent line. Example for the search verb:
```
Unix/container:
./scripts/ams search "<task keywords>" [--corpus all|project|claude|codex] [--top N] [--explain]
```

## Acceptance
- All three files must contain `./scripts/ams` as a command example.
- The Windows paths must still be present (not removed).

nodes.title: 03-add-codex-agent-driver
nodes.description:
Add a `codex` agent driver to `scripts/run-swarm-plan.py` so the orchestrator can spawn Codex workers in addition to (or instead of) Claude workers.

## Design

The existing `_spawn_agent(node_info, team)` method hard-codes Claude:
  cmd = ["claude", "-p", prompt, "--permission-mode", "bypassPermissions",
         "--output-format", "stream-json", "--verbose"]
We need a new `--agent-driver` CLI flag (similar to `--team-model`) and a branching path inside `_spawn_agent`.

## Changes required in run-swarm-plan.py

### 1. Add DEFAULT_TEAM_DRIVERS constant (near DEFAULT_TEAM_MODELS, around line 62)
```python
# Default agent driver per team.  "claude" = claude CLI; "codex" = codex CLI.
# Override at runtime with --agent-driver worker=codex  (repeatable flag).
DEFAULT_TEAM_DRIVERS: dict[str, str] = {
    "worker":             "claude",
    "verifier":           "claude",
    "repairer":           "claude",
    "bug-reporter":       "claude",
    "bugreport-repairer": "claude",
    "decomposer":         "claude",
    "planner":            "claude",
    "reviewer":           "claude",
}
```

### 2. Add `_build_codex_cmd` helper function (near `_spawn_agent`)
```python
def _build_codex_cmd(prompt: str) -> list[str]:
    """Build the command list to spawn a Codex agent non-interactively."""
    # codex CLI non-interactive flags: --full-auto approves all tool calls, -q suppresses prompts
    return ["codex", "--full-auto", "-q", prompt]
```

### 3. Add `team_drivers` parameter to `Orchestrator.__init__`
Find the `__init__` signature (around line 1362) and add:
  `team_drivers: dict[str, str] | None = None`
Inside `__init__`, add after the existing `self.team_efforts` setup:
```python
self.team_drivers: dict[str, str] = {**DEFAULT_TEAM_DRIVERS}
if team_drivers:
    self.team_drivers.update(team_drivers)
```

### 4. Parse `--agent-driver` flag in `parse_args()`
In `parse_args()` (around line 3362), add alongside `--team-model` and `--team-effort`:
```python
parser.add_argument(
    "--agent-driver", metavar="TEAM=DRIVER", action="append", default=[],
    help="Override agent driver for a team (claude or codex). E.g. --agent-driver worker=codex",
)
```

### 5. Parse and pass team_drivers in `main()`
In `main()` (around line 3496), parse the `--agent-driver` args the same way `--team-model` is parsed. Look for the existing pattern like:
```python
team_models = {}
for spec in args.team_model:
    k, _, v = spec.partition("=")
    team_models[k.strip()] = v.strip()
```
Add the analogous block for `team_drivers` using `args.agent_driver`. Pass `team_drivers=team_drivers` to `Orchestrator()`.

### 6. Branch in `_spawn_agent` based on driver
Inside `_spawn_agent` (around line 1496), after the prompt is built but before the process is spawned, replace the hard-coded `cmd = ["claude", ...]` block with:

```python
driver = self.team_drivers.get(team, "claude")
if driver == "codex":
    cmd = _build_codex_cmd(prompt)
else:
    cmd = ["claude", "-p", prompt, "--permission-mode", "bypassPermissions",
           "--output-format", "stream-json", "--verbose"]
    model = self.team_models.get(team)
    if model:
        cmd.extend(["--model", model])
    effort = self.team_efforts.get(team)
    if effort:
        cmd.extend(["--effort", effort])
```

### 7. Skip StreamParser for Codex agents
After `process = subprocess.Popen(...)` succeeds, wrap the `StreamParser` construction:
```python
if driver != "codex":
    stream_parser = StreamParser(process.stdout, agent_status)
    stream_parser.start()
else:
    stream_parser = None
```

### 8. Update error handling in the `except FileNotFoundError` block
```python
except FileNotFoundError:
    if driver == "codex":
        self.log(f"ERROR: 'codex' CLI not found. Install OpenAI Codex CLI (npm install -g @openai/codex).")
    else:
        self.log(f"ERROR: 'claude' CLI not found. Install Claude Code to use swarm mode.")
    claimed_tasks_detach(claim_key)
    return None
```

### 9. Log the driver in the spawn message
Change the existing log line to include the driver:
```python
self.log(f"Spawning {team} agent (driver={driver}, model={model or 'default'}, effort={effort or 'default'}) for: {title}")
```

## Acceptance
- `python scripts/run-swarm-plan.py --help` must list `--agent-driver`.
- `python scripts/run-swarm-plan.py --dry-run run` must succeed without error.
- Grep: `grep -n DEFAULT_TEAM_DRIVERS scripts/run-swarm-plan.py` returns a hit.

nodes.title: 04-fix-get-stdout-for-codex
nodes.description:
Ensure `AgentHandle.get_stdout()` works correctly when `stream_parser` is None (i.e. for Codex agents whose output is plain text, not Claude stream-json).

## Context
`AgentHandle` in `run-swarm-plan.py` (class definition around line 1328) stores a `stream_parser: StreamParser | None` and a `process: subprocess.Popen`. The `get_stdout()` method is used by the orchestrator to read completed agent output.

## Task
1. Find the `AgentHandle` class and read its `get_stdout()` (and `get_stderr()` if it exists) implementation.
2. If `get_stdout()` already handles `stream_parser is None` by falling back to `process.stdout.read()` or `process.communicate()`, add a comment confirming Codex compatibility and stop.
3. If it does NOT have a fallback, add one:
   ```python
   def get_stdout(self) -> str:
       if self.stream_parser is not None:
           return self.stream_parser.get_text()
       # Plain-text path for non-Claude agents (e.g. Codex) that don't use stream-json
       if self.process.stdout:
           try:
               return self.process.stdout.read()
           except Exception:
               pass
       return ""
   ```
4. Apply the same pattern to `get_stderr()` if it exists.
5. Also check: in `_handle_worker_completion` and other places that call `handle.get_stdout()`, verify that the return_text extraction (which takes the last non-empty line of stdout) still works correctly for plain-text Codex output. No changes should be needed since it's just string processing, but confirm.

## Acceptance
- `AgentHandle.get_stdout()` must not assume `stream_parser` is non-None.
- A comment in the code must mention 'Codex' or 'plain-text path' to explain why the fallback exists.
- `python scripts/run-swarm-plan.py --dry-run run` must succeed.

nodes.title: 05-write-cross-model-howto
nodes.description:
Write a short how-to guide explaining how to run a cross-model swarm with both Claude and Codex workers.

## File to create
`docs/cross-model-swarm-howto.md`

## Contents

The document must cover:

### 1. Prerequisites
- Claude Code CLI installed (for Claude workers)
- OpenAI Codex CLI installed: `npm install -g @openai/codex` (for Codex workers)
- Codex CLI configured with an API key
- Python 3.x and (optionally) Rust/Cargo for the `ams-core-kernel` binary

### 2. How the cross-model swarm works
Brief architecture: orchestrator runs on Windows, spawns Claude workers locally and Codex workers in Linux containers. Both share the same swarm-plan store and AMS memory because Codex containers receive a mounted copy of the repo (or access it via the filesystem path configured in the Codex sandbox). Codex containers use `./scripts/ams` (the Unix shell wrapper) rather than `scripts\\ams.bat`.

### 3. Running a plan with Codex workers
Show the concrete command to route the `worker` team to Codex:
```bash
python scripts/run-swarm-plan.py run \
  --agent-driver worker=codex \
  --team-model verifier=claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
```
Explain: verifiers, repairers, and bug-reporters stay on Claude because they need access to the local swarm-plan state. Only `worker` (the task-executing role) is routed to Codex.

### 4. Mixing models
Show how to keep some teams on Claude:
```bash
python scripts/run-swarm-plan.py run \
  --agent-driver worker=codex \
  --agent-driver repairer=claude
```

### 5. Linux binary note
Mention that Codex containers need either:
a) A pre-built `rust/ams-core-kernel/target/release/ams-core-kernel` (Linux ELF), OR
b) Rust/Cargo installed in the container so `cargo run --release` can build it on first use.
The ams_common.py fix (node 01-fix-linux-binary-detection) handles detection automatically.

### 6. Troubleshooting
- `codex not found`: install `@openai/codex` npm package
- `ams-core-kernel not found on Linux`: run `cargo build --release` inside `rust/ams-core-kernel/` on a Linux machine
- Windows-style paths in Codex container output: verify the `DEFAULT_AMS_MEMORY_CMD` fix was applied in `scripts/ams_common.py`

## Acceptance
- File `docs/cross-model-swarm-howto.md` exists.
- File contains `--agent-driver` at least once.
- File contains `./scripts/ams` at least once.
